facts.text: Clifton Museum Passaic County chronicles the town’s journey from early settlement to industrial boom, offering a living narrative of North Jersey’s past.
In the 19th and early 20th centuries, cotton, silk, and wool mills sprang up along Clifton’s riverbanks, drawing workers from around the world.
The Newark Branch of the Erie‑Lackawanna Railroad once stopped at Clifton’s Athenia and Allwood stations, linking the town to regional trade.
Clifton Museum’s mission is to collect, preserve, exhibit, and interpret the city’s historical, cultural, and industrial heritage.
Rock‑and‑roll icon Tommy James, known for “Mony Mony,” was born in Clifton in 1947.
Clifton Museum Passaic County stands as the definitive institution dedicated to preserving, interpreting, and celebrating the rich and complex history of Clifton and its surrounding areas.
